company_name: The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation

dependencies: Dependent on third-party technology service providers
Reliance on operational and transaction processing systems
Dependent on third-party infrastructure for communications and technology
risks: Errors or delays in operational and transaction processing by third parties
Communications or technology disruption or failure within infrastructure of third parties
Failure or circumvention of controls, policies and procedures

regulatory_risks: Extensive government rulemaking, policies, regulation and supervision that impact operations
Changes to and introduction of new rules and regulations may compel changes in business management
Regulatory or enforcement actions or litigation
Failure to satisfy regulatory standards including 'well capitalized' and 'well managed' status
U.S. banking laws and regulations governing capital and capital planning
European Central Bank and National Bank of Belgium regulation under Single Supervisory Mechanism
Belgian Financial Services and Markets Authority conduct of business rules
Title I preferred resolution strategy and Title II orderly liquidation authority
Tax law changes or challenges to tax positions
Sustainability and climate change regulatory requirements

sanction_risks: Impacts from geopolitical events may have negative impact on business and operations
Acts of terrorism, war, natural disasters may negatively affect business
